title: Exit code lost with set -e
error: Cannot capture exit code - script terminates before $?
cause: With set -e, any non-zero exit code terminates the script immediately. The line after the command is never reached.
solution: Use inline capture pattern: cmd && code=0 || code=$?
example_bad:
set -e
some_command_that_may_fail
exit_code=$?  # Never reached if command fails!
example_good:
set -e
some_command_that_may_fail && exit_code=0 || exit_code=$?
echo "Exit code: $exit_code"  # Always reached
